questions.Question: Which statement accurately describes how Microsoft 365 Copilot handles customer data in relation to Large Language Model (LLM) training?

Options.text: Data is used to train global models only if the user gives explicit consent.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Microsoft does not use customer data for global training at all.

Options.text: Data is anonymized and pooled with other tenants to improve the LLM.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Tenant isolation prevents pooling of any kind.

Options.text: Data remains within the organization's tenant and is not used to train global models.
Options.reason: Correct. This is the cornerstone of Microsoft's AI privacy commitment.

Options.text: Data is sent to OpenAI for processing and training on their public servers.
Options.reason: Incorrect. All processing happens within the Microsoft Azure/M365 boundary.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Microsoft 365 Copilot honors the same data residency and privacy boundaries as existing M365 services.

questions.Question: A manager is unable to use Copilot in an Excel workbook stored on SharePoint. What is the most likely technical reason?

Options.text: The workbook is saved in the .xlsb format.
Options.reason: Incorrect. While format matters, the most common issue is structural.

Options.text: The data has not been formatted as an Excel Table.
Options.reason: Correct. Copilot requires the 'Table' object to identify headers and rows.

Options.text: The workbook is currently being co-authored by another user.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Copilot supports co-authoring sessions.

Options.text: The user does not have 'Owner' permissions on the file.
Options.reason: Incorrect. 'Edit' permissions are sufficient for Copilot use.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Data must be converted to a Table (Ctrl+T) so Copilot can define the range for analysis.

questions.Question: In the Microsoft 365 Copilot architecture, what is the primary role of 'grounding'?

Options.text: To filter out profanity and offensive language from the user input.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is part of the safety guardrail system, not grounding.

Options.text: To connect the LLM to specific, real-time business data via Microsoft Graph.
Options.reason: Correct. Grounding provides the context needed for accurate, tenant-specific answers.

Options.text: To speed up the generation of text by using cached responses.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Grounding is about accuracy and relevance, not speed.

Options.text: To ensure the AI only uses data that is more than two years old.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Grounding allows for up-to-date data retrieval.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Grounding uses RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) to supply the LLM with relevant business facts.

questions.Question: A prompt says: 'Review the attached ProjectCharter.pdf and summarize the key milestones in a table format.' Which part represents the 'Source' pillar?

Options.text: 'Review the attached ProjectCharter.pdf'
Options.reason: Correct. The document is the source material the AI must reference.

Options.text: 'Summarize the key milestones'
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is the Goal.

Options.text: 'In a table format'
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is the Expectation.

Options.text: The professional tone of the request.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Tone is part of Context.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The Source identifies the specific data (files, emails, chats) used to ground the response.

questions.Question: How do Copilot components in Microsoft Loop facilitate team collaboration?

Options.text: By locking the page so only one person can use the AI at a time.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Loop is designed for multi-user collaboration.

Options.text: By allowing multiple users to iteratively draft and refine AI content on a shared canvas.
Options.reason: Correct. This is the core 'multiplayer' benefit of Loop.

Options.text: By automatically deleting older drafts to save space.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Version history is maintained.

Options.text: By converting all text into a static PDF once the AI finishes.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Loop pages remain interactive and live.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Loop provides a collaborative 'Copilot Page' where teams co-create with AI.

questions.Question: What is the industry term for when an AI provides a factually incorrect response that appears highly convincing?

Options.text: Model Drift
Options.reason: Incorrect. Drift refers to performance degradation over time.

Options.text: Stochastic Parroting
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is a general critique of LLMs, not this specific error.

Options.text: Hallucination
Options.reason: Correct. This is when the model predicts the next likely word based on patterns rather than facts.

Options.text: Logic Bloat
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is not a standard industry term.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Hallucinations are minimized by grounding but remain a risk inherent to LLMs.

questions.Question: You are building a custom Agent in Copilot Studio. What is the benefit of adding a 'Public Website' to the Knowledge tab?

Options.text: It allows the Agent to use that website's information to answer user queries.
Options.reason: Correct. This provides a dynamic knowledge base for the agent.

Options.text: It automatically hosts the Agent on that website.
Options.reason: Incorrect. The website is a source, not a deployment channel.

Options.text: It allows the Agent to change the content of that website.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Knowledge sources are read-only for grounding purposes.

Options.text: It bypasses the need for any user authentication.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Authentication depends on the deployment channel.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Adding websites to Knowledge allows for 'Generative Answers' based on the site content.

questions.Question: A company policy forbids the recording of meetings. How can employees still use Copilot to summarize a live discussion?

Options.text: They cannot; Copilot is disabled without a permanent recording.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Microsoft has a specific mode for this.

Options.text: By enabling 'Copilot without transcription' in the meeting settings.
Options.reason: Correct. This processes data in real-time without saving a transcript.

Options.text: By taking manual notes and pasting them into the chat later.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This defeats the purpose of the in-meeting assistant.

Options.text: By having a user record the meeting on their mobile phone.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This bypasses the corporate AI features and violates policy.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The 'Without Transcription' mode allows for transient AI processing during the meeting session.

questions.Question: When a prompt asks the AI to 'Think step-by-step before providing the final answer,' which technique is being used?

Options.text: Few-Shot Prompting
Options.reason: Incorrect. Few-shot involves providing examples.

Options.text: Chain of Thought (CoT)
Options.reason: Correct. CoT encourages the model to break down complex logic into pieces.

Options.text: Negative Prompting
Options.reason: Incorrect. Negative prompting tells the AI what NOT to do.

Options.text: Zero-Shot Prompting
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is the default state where no guidance is given.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Chain of Thought helps the AI handle complex reasoning and reduces errors.

questions.Question: In Outlook, 'Coaching by Copilot' provides feedback on which of the following areas?

Options.text: Spelling and Grammar only.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is handled by traditional Editor features.

Options.text: Tone, Sentiment, and Clarity.
Options.reason: Correct. Coaching analyzes the impact the email will have on the recipient.

Options.text: The fastest time to send the email for a quick reply.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is part of Viva Insights, not Copilot Coaching.

Options.text: Whether the recipient is currently out of the office.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is a standard Outlook feature.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Coaching provides actionable suggestions to improve the professional quality of your communication.

questions.Question: What happens if a user tries to use Copilot to summarize a document protected by a 'Highly Confidential' Purview label?

Options.text: Copilot will ignore the label and summarize the text anyway.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Copilot respects all data protections.

Options.text: The summary generated will automatically inherit the 'Highly Confidential' label.
Options.reason: Correct. This maintains the chain of compliance for generated data.

Options.text: Copilot will ask the user to remove the label before proceeding.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This would be a security risk.

Options.text: The document will be deleted automatically for security.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Label inheritance ensures that data generated from protected sources remains protected.

questions.Question: Where should a developer define the 'Personality' and 'Response Guidelines' for a custom Agent?

Options.text: The Knowledge section.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is for raw data sources.

Options.text: The Instructions section.
Options.reason: Correct. Instructions define the persona and behavioral logic.

Options.text: The Plugins section.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is for external system integrations.

Options.text: The Analytics dashboard.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This tracks performance.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Instructions act as the 'System Message' that guides every interaction the agent has.

questions.Question: Which component is responsible for checking if a user has permission to access a file before Copilot uses it for grounding?

Options.text: The Large Language Model (LLM)
Options.reason: Incorrect. The LLM has no knowledge of user permissions.

Options.text: Microsoft Graph
Options.reason: Correct. Graph manages identity and permission context.

Options.text: The Bing Search Engine
Options.reason: Incorrect. Bing handles public web data.

Options.text: The User's Desktop Client
Options.reason: Incorrect. Security is handled at the service level.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Microsoft Graph ensures that Copilot only 'sees' data the user is already authorized to view.

questions.Question: To use 'Create presentation from file,' where must the source Word document be stored?

Options.text: On the local hard drive (C: Drive).
Options.reason: Incorrect. Copilot cannot access local un-synced files.

Options.text: In a cloud location like OneDrive or SharePoint.
Options.reason: Correct. This allows Microsoft Graph to access the file content.

Options.text: On a physical USB flash drive.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Inside a password-protected .ZIP file.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This blocks AI access.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Copilot requires cloud-based file storage to bridge the gap between your data and the AI service.

questions.Question: If you ask Copilot in Word to 'Rewrite this list as a series of professional emails,' which type of capability are you using?

Options.text: Summarization
Options.reason: Incorrect. This would shorten the content.

Options.text: Transformation
Options.reason: Correct. You are changing the format and style of existing information.

Options.text: Data Extraction
Options.reason: Incorrect. Extraction finds specific facts (dates, names).

Options.text: Transcription
Options.reason: Incorrect. Transcription turns audio to text.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Transformation involves taking existing text and changing its form, tone, or medium.

questions.Question: Which Microsoft AI principle focuses on ensuring that AI systems do not reinforce existing social biases?

Options.text: Inclusiveness
Options.reason: Incorrect. This focuses on accessibility for all people.

Options.text: Fairness
Options.reason: Correct. Fairness is the specific principle targeting bias mitigation.

Options.text: Transparency
Options.reason: Incorrect. This focuses on explaining how the AI works.

Options.text: Accountability
Options.reason: Incorrect. This focuses on human oversight.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Fairness ensures AI treatments are equitable across different demographic groups.

questions.Question: What is the primary function of the 'Semantic Index' in the Copilot architecture?

Options.text: To index the public internet for global search results.
Options.reason: Incorrect. That is the role of Bing.

Options.text: To create a vector-based map of tenant data for natural language retrieval.
Options.reason: Correct. This allows Copilot to find relevant internal files contextually.

Options.text: To store user passwords securely.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Passwords are managed by Entra ID.

Options.text: To translate prompts into different languages.
Options.reason: Incorrect. The LLM handles translation.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The Semantic Index works with Microsoft Graph to surface the most relevant internal data.

questions.Question: How should a user confirm the accuracy of a complex formula generated by Copilot in Excel?

Options.text: Assume it is correct if the Copilot button is green.
Options.reason: Incorrect. AI output always requires human review.

Options.text: Use the 'Explain' or 'View Formula' option to check the logic.
Options.reason: Correct. This allows the user to verify the math before applying it.

Options.text: Cross-reference it with ChatGPT.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is inefficient and potentially insecure.

Options.text: Delete the data and start over if it looks complex.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Verification is a key part of the 'Human-in-the-loop' workflow for AI.

questions.Question: What is a major advantage of using a 'Copilot Agent' over the standard M365 Chat?

Options.text: Agents can be tailored with specific knowledge sources and pinned for easy access.
Options.reason: Correct. Agents provide specialized, repeatable value for specific roles.

Options.text: Agents do not require a Microsoft 365 license.
Options.reason: Incorrect. All Copilot features require licensing.

Options.text: Agents are the only way to use Copilot in Word.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Word has built-in Copilot features.

Options.text: Agents can predict the future price of stocks.
Options.reason: Incorrect. AI is not a crystal ball.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Agents allow for 'expert' AI assistants dedicated to specific business functions.

questions.Question: In the prompt 'Draft a project update as a senior consultant for the board,' which pillar is 'as a senior consultant'?

Options.text: Goal
Options.reason: Incorrect. The goal is the project update.

Options.text: Source
Options.reason: Incorrect. This prompt lacks a specific source link.

Options.text: Context
Options.reason: Correct. This defines the persona and professional background.

Options.text: Expectation
Options.reason: Incorrect. Expectations are usually about format or length.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Context provides the AI with the 'who' and 'why' of the request.

questions.Question: Which Copilot feature allows you to ask 'Has my name been mentioned yet?' during a live meeting?

Options.text: The Recap tab.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Recap is used after the meeting.

Options.text: The in-meeting Copilot chat pane.
Options.reason: Correct. This allows for real-time querying of the transcript.

Options.text: Teams Walkie Talkie.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Microsoft Viva.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The in-meeting pane allows users to catch up or find specific details without interrupting others.

questions.Question: In Copilot Studio, what is the role of an 'Orchestrator'?

Options.text: To record the user's voice during the chat.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: To manage the selection and execution of the best tool or knowledge source for a query.
Options.reason: Correct. It acts as the 'logic router' for the agent.

Options.text: To bill the customer for AI usage.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: To create images for the agent's profile.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The orchestrator determines how to fulfill a user's request using the available capabilities.

questions.Question: How can you refine a summary of a 50-email thread to be specifically about 'Budget Decisions'?

Options.text: You cannot; summaries are automated and cannot be changed.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Open Copilot in the side pane and ask it to summarize the thread focusing only on budget items.
Options.reason: Correct. Iterative prompting allows for specific filtering of data.

Options.text: Archive the non-budget emails first.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This is too much manual work.

Options.text: Ask the sender to rewrite the emails.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The side-pane Copilot in Outlook allows for conversational refinement of email summaries.

questions.Question: What is 'Prompt Injection'?

Options.text: A way to make the AI respond faster by using more RAM.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: An attempt to manipulate the AI into ignoring its safety filters or revealing restricted data.
Options.reason: Correct. This is a common security challenge for LLMs.

Options.text: A technique to insert images into a text prompt.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Updating the Copilot software automatically.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Prompt injection involves crafting inputs that try to bypass the AI's intended instructions.

questions.Question: A team is using a Copilot Page in Loop. One member does not have a Copilot license. What is their experience?

Options.text: They cannot see the page at all.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Access to the page is based on Loop permissions.

Options.text: They can view and edit text on the page but cannot trigger the AI to generate new content.
Options.reason: Correct. Basic collaboration is free; AI features require a license.

Options.text: They are automatically granted a temporary license.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: The page becomes read-only for everyone.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Microsoft separates collaborative access from generative AI feature access.

questions.Question: What scenario is best suited for using Copilot in 'Notebook' mode rather than 'Chat' mode?

Options.text: Checking a quick weather update.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Developing a complex, long-form document through multiple iterations of a prompt.
Options.reason: Correct. Notebook provides more space and a side-by-side view for drafting.

Options.text: Sending a quick message to a teammate.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Use Teams for this.

Options.text: Searching for a file in SharePoint.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Notebook is designed for iterative, high-precision prompt engineering.

questions.Question: If a user asks Copilot to 'Compare my last three sales emails with the company pricing sheet,' which data is used for grounding?

Options.text: Only the public website of the company.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: The user's sent emails and the specific document in SharePoint.
Options.reason: Correct. Both are internal data points accessed via Microsoft Graph.

Options.text: General sales data from the internet.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: The user's browsing history from personal devices.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Personal data is never used.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Copilot grounds responses using the user's accessible enterprise data.

questions.Question: Which administrator role has the highest authority to enable 'Optional Connected Experiences' like Web Search for Copilot?

Options.text: Helpdesk Administrator
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: User Administrator
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Global Administrator
Options.reason: Correct. Global Admins manage tenant-wide service settings.

Options.text: Billing Administrator
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Tenant-level privacy and connectivity settings require Global Admin privileges.

questions.Question: When you upload a photo of a whiteboard to Copilot, it can turn the handwritten notes into a Word document. What technology is primarily responsible for this?

Options.text: Blockchain
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Optical Character Recognition (OCR)
Options.reason: Correct. OCR interprets text within images.

Options.text: Predictive Analytics
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Data Mining
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: OCR is the core technology used to digitize visual text for AI processing.

questions.Question: A prompt states: 'Give me a summary in 3 bullet points, using a friendly tone.' Which pillar is '3 bullet points'?

Options.text: Context
Options.reason: Incorrect. Tone is context, but bullet points are format.

Options.text: Expectation
Options.reason: Correct. This defines the format and constraints of the output.

Options.text: Goal
Options.reason: Incorrect. The goal is the summary itself.

Options.text: Source
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Expectations dictate how the final result should look and behave.

questions.Question: Why is it important to ensure a column in Excel contains only one type of data (e.g., all numbers) before using Copilot?

Options.text: Copilot will crash if it sees a word in a number column.
Options.reason: Incorrect. It won't crash, but it will fail the analysis.

Options.text: Inconsistent data types prevent accurate mathematical and trend modeling.
Options.reason: Correct. Clean data is required for the AI to perform calculations correctly.

Options.text: Mixed data types consume too much electricity.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: It is a requirement of the Excel license.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: AI analysis relies on structured, consistent data sets.

questions.Question: Where does Copilot search if you ask it a question about current world events not found in your company files?

Options.text: It checks the internal Semantic Index only.
Options.reason: Incorrect. This only contains company data.

Options.text: It uses Bing Search (if enabled by an admin).
Options.reason: Correct. This provides public, real-time web grounding.

Options.text: It asks a Global Admin for the answer.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: It guesses based on data from 2021.
Options.reason: Incorrect. Grounding allows for current results.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Bing Search provides the 'world knowledge' that the internal Graph does not contain.

questions.Question: In 'Draft a response to this customer complaint based on our refund policy,' what is the 'refund policy'?

Options.text: The Source
Options.reason: Correct. It is the specific data point the AI must use for the answer.

Options.text: The Goal
Options.reason: Incorrect. The goal is the drafted response.

Options.text: The Context
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: The Expectation
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The source provides the 'facts' for the AI's grounding.

questions.Question: What does a 'Plugin' allow a custom Copilot Agent to do?

Options.text: Change the background color of Teams.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Perform actions in external systems, such as checking an order status in a CRM.
Options.reason: Correct. Plugins allow for functional integration.

Options.text: Delete old files automatically.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Block users from using the AI.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Plugins give the AI 'hands' to perform tasks beyond just speaking.

questions.Question: A user asks for a summary of a meeting that occurred yesterday, but Copilot says it cannot find it. Why?

Options.text: Copilot forgets everything after 1 hour.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Transcription or recording was never started during the meeting.
Options.reason: Correct. Copilot requires the transcript as its source data.

Options.text: The meeting was too short (under 5 minutes).
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: The user was on mute for most of the meeting.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: No transcript means no source for the AI to summarize.

questions.Question: Which principle requires developers to provide information about the limitations and operational details of an AI system?

Options.text: Privacy & Security
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Transparency
Options.reason: Correct. Transparency involves being open about how the system works.

Options.text: Fairness
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Reliability
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Transparency helps users understand when and how to trust AI output.

questions.Question: Why might a 'Work' chat Copilot fail to find a document that you just uploaded to your personal OneDrive (consumer version)?

Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Personal OneDrive is faster than Copilot.

Options.text: You need to pay for a personal AI license separately.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Tenant boundaries prevent data leakage between consumer and commercial accounts.

questions.Question: When Copilot provides a summary with small numbers (e.g., [1], [2]) next to sentences, what are these?

Options.text: The difficulty level of the sentence.

Options.text: The amount of time it took to generate that sentence.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Citations are the primary tool for verifying AI grounding.

questions.Question: What is the result of using the 'Organize Slides' command in PowerPoint?

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Organization helps manage large presentations by creating logical themes.

questions.Question: If a company wants to ensure Copilot NEVER uses the public internet to answer questions, what should the Admin do?

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Admins have granular control over external data connectivity.

Options.text: Windows Notepad
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Loop's design allows for collaborative AI usage within a team workspace.

questions.Question: In 'Rewrite this to be more professional,' which pillar is 'more professional'?

Options.text: Expectation
Options.reason: Incorrect. This describes tone, which is context.

Options.text: Context
Options.reason: Correct. Tone and persona instructions provide the context for the style.

questions.Question: What is the function of the 'Knowledge' tab in a custom Agent?

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Knowledge grounding makes an agent specialized and accurate.

questions.Question: You receive a 'Coaching' tip saying your email 'might be perceived as aggressive.' What is the AI's role here?

Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Coaching empowers the user to communicate more effectively without taking over the task.

questions.Question: How does M365 Copilot handle data that has been labeled with 'No Forwarding' or encryption?

Options.text: It honors the encryption and only allows Copilot to process it if the user has the right to view it.

Options.text: It makes the file public to everyone.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Privacy and security labels are deep-coded into the Copilot workflow.

questions.Question: 'Create a summary that is exactly 100 words.' Which pillar is 'exactly 100 words'?

Options.text: Goal
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Expectation
Options.reason: Correct. This is a specific constraint on the output.

Options.text: Context
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Source
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Expectations provide the boundaries for the AI's final response.

questions.Question: What prevents a user at 'Company A' from using Copilot to see files from 'Company B'?

Options.text: The fact that they are in different countries.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Logical Tenant Isolation within the Microsoft 365 cloud architecture.

Options.text: They use different versions of Windows.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Tenant isolation is a fundamental security feature of M365.

questions.Question: Where can you find the AI-generated list of 'Action Items' after a Teams meeting?

Options.text: In your personal email inbox.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: In the 'Recap' tab of the meeting record.

Options.text: In the Windows Recycle Bin.

Options.text: On the company's public LinkedIn page.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: The Recap tab is the central hub for post-meeting AI insights.

questions.Question: What is a prerequisite for Copilot in Excel to help with 'What-if' scenarios?

questions.Question: A custom Agent keeps talking about things it shouldn't. How do you restrict its behavior?

Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Update the 'Instructions' with negative constraints (e.g., 'Do not discuss X').

Options.text: Ask the AI to be nicer.

Options.text: Change the Agent's icon.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Instructions are the most powerful tool for shaping Agent behavior.

questions.Question: In RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), what does 'Retrieval' refer to?

Options.text: Fetching relevant data from your files to give the LLM context.
Options.reason: Correct. This 'retrieves' the facts before the 'generation' happens.

Options.text: Deleting old emails.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

Options.text: Downloading the Copilot app.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Retrieval ensures the AI is not just guessing but using your actual data.

questions.Question: Can Copilot see your personal browser history in Edge if you are logged into your work profile?

Options.text: Only on Tuesdays.
Options.reason: Incorrect.

questions.CorrectAnswerExplanation: Data privacy boundaries are strictly enforced between identity profiles.

questions.Question: If a user wants to highlight all sales over $500 in red, how can Copilot help?
